intro: Diplodium × toveyanum is a hybrid orchid species, resulting from the cross between Diplodium cucullifolium and Diplodium robustum, both native to Australia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its purported health benefits, including its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems. The bioactive constituents of Diplodium × toveyanum include glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Diplodium × toveyanum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness the plant's potential health benefits.
